intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated ingredient lists (items flow onto screen) over instant static lists, especially when scanning recipes quickly
facade.label: Ingredients slide in
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use entrance animations for recipe components (ingredients, steps, times) to create momentum and reduce cognitive load of static walls of text
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer instant, scannable layouts without animation overhead—keep ingredient lists static and immediately readable

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a friendly, supportive tone that makes them feel capable rather than intimidated
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and encouraging
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use conversational language, celebratory moments, and reassurance. Tone like a supportive friend, not a culinary authority.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more straightforward, neutral, or even playfully irreverent tone. They don't want hand-holding or motivational language.

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer animated ingredient lists (items flow onto screen) over instant static lists, especially when scanning recipes quickly
evidence.implication: The app should use entrance animations for recipe components (ingredients, steps, times) to create momentum and reduce cognitive load of static walls of text

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to inviting, approachable visual design that feels encouraging rather than clinical or sterile
facade.axisTargeted: Emotional tone via color saturation and warmth
facade.label: Warm colorful cards
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into vibrant, saturated palettes and soft, rounded card layouts to make cooking feel less intimidating and more playful
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward cooler, more minimal, or monochromatic design language that prioritizes simplicity and clarity over emotional warmth

facade.hypothesis: Users with cooking anxiety prefer seeing single metrics (cook time, servings) over dense ingredient tables
facade.label: One number, big
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe app should lead with ONE scannable metric per card (prep time, difficulty level, or yield) before showing full ingredient lists
facade.rejectImplies: Users want all recipe details visible at once; dense tables and complete data hierarchy matter more than progressive disclosure

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to soft, approachable, non-intimidating visual warmth than stark minimalism
facade.label: Warm beige cards
facade.acceptImplies: The app should lean into comfort and gentleness—think soft color palettes, rounded corners, and a 'kitchen as a friend' vibe rather than 'kitchen as a gym'
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer either cooler, cleaner aesthetics (modern/minimal) OR they don't care about warmth at all—they want speed and efficiency over emotional comfort

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, time, skill) upfront vs. minimal friction and discovery-based browsing
Whether user wants instant gratification with auto-play timers and notifications vs. hands-off, self-paced exploration
axes.poleA: Alerts and timers or silent mode

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, time, skill) upfront vs. minimal friction and discovery-based browsing
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant gratification with auto-play timers and notifications vs. hands-off, self-paced exploration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: For recipe-haters, hiding complexity in a collapsible sidebar reduces cognitive load and keeps the active task centered
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: sidebar navigation vs. full-width single-task focus
facade.label: Sidebar with collapsed steps
facade.acceptImplies: User values compact, modular layouts where they control what's visible; may prefer power-user density over hand-holding
facade.rejectImplies: User wants one thing at a time, full focus on current step; finds sidebars cluttered or distracting; prefers linear, immersive flows

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking need visual pacing and sequential guidance rather than all-at-once instructions; animated step reveals reduce overwhelm and create a sense of manageable progression
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — sequential animation vs instant visibility
facade.label: Steps fade in one by one
facade.content: Steps fade in one by one
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe UI should use staggered entrance animations for cooking steps to break down complex processes into digestible moments and reduce cooking anxiety
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer seeing the full recipe at once without animations; motion feels distracting or condescending when someone is already stressed about cooking

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for Cooking Haters
draft.summary: A recipe app designed for people who want delicious meals with minimal effort. Features ultra-simple recipes, ingredient shortcuts, and time-saving hacks.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Recipes for people who hate cooking

Quick Wins
5-Minute Pasta Aglio e Olio
5 min • 2 ingredients
Garlic, olive oil, pasta. That's it. Restaurant-quality in your kitchen.
No-Cook Caprese Salad
3 min • 4 ingredients
Tomato, mozzarella, basil, olive oil. Slice and serve.
Shortcuts & Hacks
Use Rotisserie Chicken
Time-saver
Skip the cooking. Shred it for tacos, salads, grain bowls, or pasta.
Pre-Cut Vegetables
Effort-saver
Buy them. Your time is worth more than the extra cost.
Get Started
Browse recipes by time, ingredients, or mood. Save your favorites. Never stress about dinner again.

Whether user needs heavy filtering (dietary, time, skill) upfront vs. minimal friction and discovery-based browsing
Whether user wants instant gratification with auto-play timers and notifications vs. hands-off, self-paced exploration

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for Cooking Haters
draft.summary: A recipe app designed for people who want delicious meals with minimal effort. Features ultra-simple recipes, ingredient shortcuts, and time-saving hacks.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Recipes for people who hate cooking

Quick Wins
5-Minute Pasta Aglio e Olio
5 min • 2 ingredients
Garlic, olive oil, pasta. That's it. Restaurant-quality in your kitchen.
No-Cook Caprese Salad
3 min • 4 ingredients
Tomato, mozzarella, basil, olive oil. Slice and serve.
Shortcuts & Hacks
Use Rotisserie Chicken
Time-saver
Skip the cooking. Shred it for tacos, salads, grain bowls, or pasta.
Pre-Cut Vegetables
Effort-saver
Buy them. Your time is worth more than the extra cost.
Get Started
Browse recipes by time, ingredients, or mood. Save your favorites. Never stress about dinner again.
